Read smarter.

Lumio is the smart reading companion that captures highlights, auto-summarizes long articles, and quietly ties it all together with spaced recall, so the best parts of what you read actually stick.

Reading session, 14 min focus mode

The Quiet Return of Long Attention

Aeon, 4,300 words, by Maya Delacroix

The counter-movement is not nostalgia for paper, but a renegotiation with depth itself. Readers are rebuilding rituals that protect the reach of a single idea long enough to let it take root.

highlighted 3 notes linked

This idea connects to your note from Crawford, Shop Class as Soulcraft.

Trusted by readers at

Stanford
MIT Media Lab
The Atlantic

Tokopedia

Features

Built for the way you actually read.

No more highlights that die in a scrapbook. Lumio quietly turns every reading session into a growing atlas of ideas you can lean on.

Auto summaries that respect the text.

Paste a link or forward an email, Lumio returns a three-paragraph summary keyed to the author's own phrasing, with the passages worth re-reading kept intact.

  • Long reads, PDFs, newsletters, Substacks
  • Preserves quotations, no paraphrase drift
  • Tone aware, Essay vs Paper vs Brief modes

Reading Atlas, ideas that know each other.

Every highlight joins a quiet graph of concepts. When a new article touches an old one, Lumio surfaces the link in context, no tagging chore required.

  • Cross-source concept linking
  • Side-by-side quote comparison
  • Export to Obsidian, Notion, or plain Markdown

Spaced recall, without the flashcard guilt.

Lumio picks two or three short prompts a day from what you actually underlined. No cards to make, no backlog to tame, just five quiet minutes that keep ideas alive.

  • Adaptive interval, Ebbinghaus plus your pace
  • Morning commute mode, audio only
  • Skip without penalty, Lumio learns your tempo

Focus sessions that don't moralize.

Start a session, Lumio hides the noise, tracks your reading pace, and hands back a gentle recap. Stats if you want them, silence if you don't.

  • Per-source pace, not just words per minute
  • Distraction-free web reader overlay
  • Weekly reading letter, sent to your inbox

How it works

Three quiet habits. One larger mind.

You don't change what you read. Lumio changes what happens after.

  1. 01

    Save anything in one tap.

    Browser extension, iOS share sheet, email forward, RSS. Lumio meets your reading where it already happens.

  2. 02

    Highlight like you always do.

    Lumio's reader shows a clean column with your marks held in place. The Atlas learns quietly in the background.

  3. 03

    Five minutes a day, the rest stays with you.

    A short recall prompt in the morning, a quiet weekly letter on Sunday. Everything else waits for when you need it.

Pricing

Start free. Upgrade when it earns its keep.

No seat minimums, no onboarding call, cancel from the settings page in a single click.

Reader

For the curious habit.

$0 /month
  • 25 saved articles per month
  • Auto summary, Brief mode
  • Daily recall, 2 cards
  • Export to Markdown

Deep Reader

For the regular reader who wants it to stick.

$8 /month
  • Unlimited saves and summaries
  • Reading Atlas, cross-source links
  • Spaced recall, all modes
  • Weekly reading letter
  • Obsidian and Notion sync

Studio

For teams that read together.

$24 /seat / month
  • Everything in Deep Reader
  • Shared Atlases and reading circles
  • SSO, audit log, admin roles
  • Weekly digest for the whole team
Start a Studio

Questions worth asking

Frequently, and honestly, asked.

Is my reading data trained on? +

No. Your highlights, summaries, and recall history never leave your workspace, and are not used to train Lumio or any third-party model. Summary inference runs on Anthropic endpoints with zero data retention enabled.

What sources can I import from? +

Web articles via the browser extension, PDFs up to 300 pages, ePub books, Kindle highlights, Substack emails, RSS feeds, and direct paste. On the Deep Reader plan, Instapaper and Pocket archives migrate in a single click.

Will recall feel like homework? +

Two cards a morning, skippable without penalty, and Lumio learns your tempo. If a topic no longer earns its five seconds, we quietly retire it. It is designed to feel like picking up where you left off, not homework.

Can I leave with my data? +

Yes, always. One-click export of every highlight, note, and summary into portable Markdown, JSON, or a ready-made Obsidian vault. There is no lock-in, we think that makes us try harder.

Start a quieter relationship with what you read.

Free to try, five minutes to get set up, and the atlas is yours to keep either way.
